Using the vowel space as an intermediary evaluation helps understand what sounds are to be added to the training database and build speech synthesis models based on linguistics knowledge. 随着机器学习语音合成的最新发展，本研究探索结合语言学知识来可视化和评估合成语音模型训练。如果可以在合成语音中看到和听到第一和第二共振峰（进而元音空间）的变化，则这些知识可以为语音合成技术开发人员提供信息。在大型通用美国英语数据库上训练的语音合成模型被微调为新西兰英语语音，以确定是否可以看到和听到合成语音的元音空间的变化。分析微调期间不同间隔的元音空间，以确定模型是否学习了新西兰英语元音空间。我们基于元音空间分析的发现表明，我们可以可视化语音合成模型如何学习其所训练的数据库的元音空间。感知测试证实，当语音合成模型了解其正在训练的语音数据库的特征时，人类可以感知。使用元音空间作为中介评估有助于理解哪些声音需要添加到训练数据库中，并基于语言学知识构建语音合成模型。 本论文通过元音空间可视化分析文本到语音（TTS）系统的模型训练过程，探索语言学知识在合成语音评估中的应用。 Binu Abeysinghe,Jesin James,Catherine I. Watson,Felix Marattukalam eess.AS,cs.SD

authors_text: Pengcheng He,Baolin Peng,Liyang Lu,Song Wang,Jie Mei,Yang Liu,Ruochen Xu,Hany Hassan Awadalla,Yu Shi,Chenguang Zhu,Wayne Xiong,Michael Zeng,Jianfeng Gao,Xuedong Huang
title: Z-Code++: A Pre-trained Language Model Optimized for Abstractive Summarization
title_zh: Z-Code++：针对抽象摘要优化的预训练语言模型
comment: 16 pages, 3 figures. Accepted as long paper in main conference of ACL 2023
comments: 16 pages, 3 figures. Accepted as long paper in main conference of ACL 2023
abstract: This paper presents Z-Code++, a new pre-trained language model optimized for abstractive text summarization. The model extends the state of the art encoder-decoder model using three techniques. First, we use a two-phase pre-training process to improve model's performance on low-resource summarization tasks. The model is first pre-trained using text corpora for language understanding, and then is continually pre-trained on summarization corpora for grounded text generation. Second, we replace self-attention layers in the encoder with disentangled attention layers, where each word is represented using two vectors that encode its content and position, respectively. Third, we use fusion-in-encoder, a simple yet effective method of encoding long sequences in a hierarchical manner. Z-Code++ creates new state of the art on 9 out of 13 text summarization tasks across 5 languages. Our model is parameter-efficient in that it outperforms the 600x larger PaLM-540B on XSum, and the finetuned 200x larger GPT3-175B on SAMSum. In zero-shot and few-shot settings, our model substantially outperforms the competing models.
summary: This paper presents Z-Code++, a new pre-trained language model optimized for abstractive text summarization. The model extends the state of the art encoder-decoder model using three techniques. First, we use a two-phase pre-training process to improve model's performance on low-resource summarization tasks. The model is first pre-trained using text corpora for language understanding, and then is continually pre-trained on summarization corpora for grounded text generation. Second, we replace self-attention layers in the encoder with disentangled attention layers, where each word is represented using two vectors that encode its content and position, respectively. Third, we use fusion-in-encoder, a simple yet effective method of encoding long sequences in a hierarchical manner. Z-Code++ creates new state of the art on 9 out of 13 text summarization tasks across 5 languages. Our model is parameter-efficient in that it outperforms the 600x larger PaLM-540B on XSum, and the finetuned 200x larger GPT3-175B on SAMSum. In zero-shot and few-shot settings, our model substantially outperforms the competing models.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了 Z-Code++，一种针对抽象文本摘要而优化的新型预训练语言模型。该模型使用三种技术扩展了最先进的编码器-解码器模型。首先，我们使用两阶段预训练过程来提高模型在低资源摘要任务上的性能。该模型首先使用用于语言理解的文本语料库进行预训练，然后不断地使用摘要语料库进行预训练以生成基础文本。其次，我们用解纠缠的注意力层替换编码器中的自注意力层，其中每个单词使用分别编码其内容和位置的两个向量来表示。第三，我们使用编码器融合，这是一种以分层方式编码长序列的简单而有效的方法。 Z-Code++ 在跨 5 种语言的 13 个文本摘要任务中的 9 个上创造了新的技术水平。我们的模型具有参数高效性，因为它在 XSum 上的性能优于大 600 倍的 PaLM-540B，在 SAMSum 上的性能优于大 200 倍的微调 GPT3-175B。在零样本和少样本设置中，我们的模型大大优于竞争模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Z-Code++，一种优化生成式摘要的预训练语言模型，通过改进预训练流程和注意力机制提升性能。
AI.motivation: 现有模型在低资源摘要任务中表现不足，且大模型参数效率低下，需开发更高效的小参数模型以解决这一问题。
AI.method: 采用两阶段预训练（语言理解+摘要生成）、解耦注意力层（分离内容与位置向量）及融合编码器的层次化长序列编码方法。
AI.result: 在13个语言任务中9项创最优，参数规模仅为竞品1/600-1/200时性能更优，零样本/少样本场景显著超越对比模型。
AI.conclusion: Z-Code++通过创新架构设计实现了参数高效且跨语言的生成式摘要能力，为低资源场景提供有效解决方案。

title: ReckOn: A 28nm Sub-mm2 Task-Agnostic Spiking Recurrent Neural Network Processor Enabling On-Chip Learning over Second-Long Timescales
title_zh: ReckOn：28nm Sub-mm2 任务无关尖峰循环神经网络处理器，可实现秒长时间尺度的片上学习
comment: Published in the 2022 IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC), 2022
comments: Published in the 2022 IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC), 2022
abstract: A robust real-world deployment of autonomous edge devices requires on-chip adaptation to user-, environment- and task-induced variability. Due to on-chip memory constraints, prior learning devices were limited to static stimuli with no temporal contents. We propose a 0.45-mm$^2$ spiking RNN processor enabling task-agnostic online learning over seconds, which we demonstrate for navigation, gesture recognition, and keyword spotting within a 0.8-% memory overhead and a <150-$μ$W training power budget.
summary: A robust real-world deployment of autonomous edge devices requires on-chip adaptation to user-, environment- and task-induced variability. Due to on-chip memory constraints, prior learning devices were limited to static stimuli with no temporal contents. We propose a 0.45-mm$^2$ spiking RNN processor enabling task-agnostic online learning over seconds, which we demonstrate for navigation, gesture recognition, and keyword spotting within a 0.8-% memory overhead and a <150-$μ$W training power budget.
abstract_zh: 自主边缘设备在现实世界中的稳健部署需要片上适应用户、环境和任务引起的变化。由于片上内存的限制，先前的学习设备仅限于没有时间内容的静态刺激。我们提出了一种 0.45-mm$^2$ 尖峰 RNN 处理器，可在几秒钟内实现与任务无关的在线学习，我们在 0.8% 的内存开销和 <150-$μ$W 的训练功率预算内演示了导航、手势识别和关键字识别。

AI.tldr: 设计一种28nm、亚平方毫米的通用型脉冲循环神经网络处理器，实现芯片级长时间尺度在线学习。
AI.motivation: 现有边缘设备因内存限制无法处理含时序信息的动态输入，难以适应环境与任务变化。
AI.method: 开发基于脉冲神经网络的通用处理器架构，在0.8%内存开销和150μW功耗下实现秒级时序学习。
AI.result: 在导航、手势识别和关键词检测任务中验证处理器有效性，占用面积0.45mm²。
AI.conclusion: 该处理器为边缘设备实时适应复杂环境提供了低功耗、高能效的硬件解决方案。

title: Artifact-Based Domain Generalization of Skin Lesion Models
title_zh: 皮肤损伤模型的基于伪影的域概括
comment: Accepted to the ISIC Skin Image Analysis Workshop @ ECCV 2022
comments: Accepted to the ISIC Skin Image Analysis Workshop @ ECCV 2022
abstract: Deep Learning failure cases are abundant, particularly in the medical area. Recent studies in out-of-distribution generalization have advanced considerably on well-controlled synthetic datasets, but they do not represent medical imaging contexts. We propose a pipeline that relies on artifacts annotation to enable generalization evaluation and debiasing for the challenging skin lesion analysis context. First, we partition the data into levels of increasingly higher biased training and test sets for better generalization assessment. Then, we create environments based on skin lesion artifacts to enable domain generalization methods. Finally, after robust training, we perform a test-time debiasing procedure, reducing spurious features in inference images. Our experiments show our pipeline improves performance metrics in biased cases, and avoids artifacts when using explanation methods. Still, when evaluating such models in out-of-distribution data, they did not prefer clinically-meaningful features. Instead, performance only improved in test sets that present similar artifacts from training, suggesting models learned to ignore the known set of artifacts. Our results raise a concern that debiasing models towards a single aspect may not be enough for fair skin lesion analysis.
summary: Deep Learning failure cases are abundant, particularly in the medical area. Recent studies in out-of-distribution generalization have advanced considerably on well-controlled synthetic datasets, but they do not represent medical imaging contexts. We propose a pipeline that relies on artifacts annotation to enable generalization evaluation and debiasing for the challenging skin lesion analysis context. First, we partition the data into levels of increasingly higher biased training and test sets for better generalization assessment. Then, we create environments based on skin lesion artifacts to enable domain generalization methods. Finally, after robust training, we perform a test-time debiasing procedure, reducing spurious features in inference images. Our experiments show our pipeline improves performance metrics in biased cases, and avoids artifacts when using explanation methods. Still, when evaluating such models in out-of-distribution data, they did not prefer clinically-meaningful features. Instead, performance only improved in test sets that present similar artifacts from training, suggesting models learned to ignore the known set of artifacts. Our results raise a concern that debiasing models towards a single aspect may not be enough for fair skin lesion analysis.
abstract_zh: 深度学习失败的案例非常多，特别是在医疗领域。最近的分布外泛化研究在控制良好的合成数据集上取得了相当大的进展，但它们并不代表医学成像背景。我们提出了一种依赖于伪影注释的管道，以针对具有挑战性的皮肤病变分析环境进行泛化评估和去偏。首先，我们将数据划分为越来越高偏差的训练和测试集级别，以实现更好的泛化评估。然后，我们创建基于皮肤病变伪影的环境以启用域泛化方法。最后，经过稳健的训练，我们执行测试时去偏过程，减少推理图像中的虚假特征。我们的实验表明，我们的管道在有偏差的情况下提高了性能指标，并在使用解释方法时避免了伪影。尽管如此，当在分布外数据中评估此类模型时，他们并不喜欢有临床意义的特征。相反，只有在训练中呈现类似工件的测试集中，性能才会有所提高，这表明模型学会了忽略已知的工件集。我们的结果引起了人们的担忧，即针对单一方面的去偏模型可能不足以进行公平的皮肤病变分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于伪影标注的领域泛化方法，用于改进皮肤病变模型的泛化能力与去偏效果。
AI.motivation: 现有分布外泛化研究多依赖合成数据集，难以应对医学影像中复杂的伪影干扰，需针对性提升皮肤病变分析的公平性。
AI.method: 通过分层划分训练/测试数据集、构建伪影驱动的领域环境、测试时执行去偏处理，使模型忽略伪影并聚焦临床特征。
AI.result: 方法在有偏数据中提升性能指标，但模型仅在测试集存在训练伪影时表现优异，未有效利用临床相关特征。
AI.conclusion: 单一维度的去偏策略不足以实现公平的皮肤病变分析，需探索更全面的领域泛化方法。

search_text: Artifact-Based Domain Generalization of Skin Lesion Models 皮肤损伤模型的基于伪影的域概括 Deep Learning failure cases are abundant, particularly in the medical area. Recent studies in out-of-distribution generalization have advanced considerably on well-controlled synthetic datasets, but they do not represent medical imaging contexts. We propose a pipeline that relies on artifacts annotation to enable generalization evaluation and debiasing for the challenging skin lesion analysis context. First, we partition the data into levels of increasingly higher biased training and test sets for better generalization assessment. Then, we create environments based on skin lesion artifacts to enable domain generalization methods. Finally, after robust training, we perform a test-time debiasing procedure, reducing spurious features in inference images. Our experiments show our pipeline improves performance metrics in biased cases, and avoids artifacts when using explanation methods. Still, when evaluating such models in out-of-distribution data, they did not prefer clinically-meaningful features. Instead, performance only improved in test sets that present similar artifacts from training, suggesting models learned to ignore the known set of artifacts. Our results raise a concern that debiasing models towards a single aspect may not be enough for fair skin lesion analysis. 深度学习失败的案例非常多，特别是在医疗领域。最近的分布外泛化研究在控制良好的合成数据集上取得了相当大的进展，但它们并不代表医学成像背景。我们提出了一种依赖于伪影注释的管道，以针对具有挑战性的皮肤病变分析环境进行泛化评估和去偏。首先，我们将数据划分为越来越高偏差的训练和测试集级别，以实现更好的泛化评估。然后，我们创建基于皮肤病变伪影的环境以启用域泛化方法。最后，经过稳健的训练，我们执行测试时去偏过程，减少推理图像中的虚假特征。我们的实验表明，我们的管道在有偏差的情况下提高了性能指标，并在使用解释方法时避免了伪影。尽管如此，当在分布外数据中评估此类模型时，他们并不喜欢有临床意义的特征。相反，只有在训练中呈现类似工件的测试集中，性能才会有所提高，这表明模型学会了忽略已知的工件集。我们的结果引起了人们的担忧，即针对单一方面的去偏模型可能不足以进行公平的皮肤病变分析。 本文提出基于伪影标注的领域泛化方法，用于改进皮肤病变模型的泛化能力与去偏效果。 Alceu Bissoto,Catarina Barata,Eduardo Valle,Sandra Avila cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Zhuowen Zhao,Tanny Chavez,Elizabeth A. Holman,Guanhua Hao,Adam Green,Harinarayan Krishnan,Dylan McReynolds,Ronald Pandolfi,Eric J. Roberts,Petrus H. Zwart,Howard Yanxon,Nicholas Schwarz,Subramanian Sankaranarayanan,Sergei V. Kalinin,Apurva Mehta,Stuart Campbell,Alexander Hexemer
title: MLExchange: A web-based platform enabling exchangeable machine learning workflows for scientific studies
title_zh: MLExchange：一个基于网络的平台，支持科学研究的可交换机器学习工作流程
comment: The accepted version with DOI and IEEE copyright notice in the first page
comments: The accepted version with DOI and IEEE copyright notice in the first page
abstract: Machine learning (ML) algorithms are showing a growing trend in helping the scientific communities across different disciplines and institutions to address large and diverse data problems. However, many available ML tools are programmatically demanding and computationally costly. The MLExchange project aims to build a collaborative platform equipped with enabling tools that allow scientists and facility users who do not have a profound ML background to use ML and computational resources in scientific discovery. At the high level, we are targeting a full user experience where managing and exchanging ML algorithms, workflows, and data are readily available through web applications. Since each component is an independent container, the whole platform or its individual service(s) can be easily deployed at servers of different scales, ranging from a personal device (laptop, smart phone, etc.) to high performance clusters (HPC) accessed (simultaneously) by many users. Thus, MLExchange renders flexible using scenarios -- users could either access the services and resources from a remote server or run the whole platform or its individual service(s) within their local network.

abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 算法在帮助不同学科和机构的科学界解决大型且多样化的数据问题方面呈现出日益增长的趋势。然而，许多可用的机器学习工具在编程上要求很高并且计算成本很高。 MLExchange 项目旨在构建一个配备支持工具的协作平台，让没有深厚 ML 背景的科学家和设施用户能够在科学发现中使用 ML 和计算资源。在较高层面上，我们的目标是提供完整的用户体验，通过 Web 应用程序可以轻松管理和交换 ML 算法、工作流程和数据。由于每个组件都是独立的容器，因此整个平台或其单个服务可以轻松部署在不同规模的服务器上，范围从个人设备（笔记本电脑、智能手机等）到由许多用户（同时）访问的高性能集群（HPC）。因此，MLExchange 提供了灵活的使用场景——用户可以从远程服务器访问服务和资源，也可以在本地网络内运行整个平台或其单个服务。

AI.tldr: 开发一个基于网络的平台，促进可交换的机器学习工作流，以支持科学研究。
AI.motivation: 现有机器学习工具对编程能力要求高且计算成本高，限制了非专业用户的使用，需要一个更易用的平台来促进科学发现。
AI.method: 构建协作平台，提供网页应用管理ML算法、工作流和数据，采用独立容器设计，支持从个人设备到高性能集群的灵活部署。
AI.result: 实现了MLExchange平台，验证了其在不同规模服务器上的灵活性和用户友好性，支持科学发现中的ML应用。
AI.conclusion: MLExchange平台有效降低了机器学习使用的门槛，促进了跨学科合作，并提供了灵活的部署选项，适用于多种计算环境。

authors_text: Jiaqi Jiang,Guanqun Cao,Aaron Butterworth,Thanh-Toan Do,Shan Luo
title: Where Shall I Touch? Vision-Guided Tactile Poking for Transparent Object Grasping
title_zh: 我应该触摸哪里？用于抓取透明物体的视觉引导触觉戳
comment: 11 pages, 11 figures, accepted by T-Mech
comments: 11 pages, 11 figures, accepted by T-Mech
abstract: Picking up transparent objects is still a challenging task for robots. The visual properties of transparent objects such as reflection and refraction make the current grasping methods that rely on camera sensing fail to detect and localise them. However, humans can handle the transparent object well by first observing its coarse profile and then poking an area of interest to get a fine profile for grasping. Inspired by this, we propose a novel framework of vision-guided tactile poking for transparent objects grasping. In the proposed framework, a segmentation network is first used to predict the horizontal upper regions named as poking regions, where the robot can poke the object to obtain a good tactile reading while leading to minimal disturbance to the object's state. A poke is then performed with a high-resolution GelSight tactile sensor. Given the local profiles improved with the tactile reading, a heuristic grasp is planned for grasping the transparent object. To mitigate the limitations of real-world data collection and labelling for transparent objects, a large-scale realistic synthetic dataset was constructed. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed segmentation network can predict the potential poking region with a high mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.360, and the vision-guided tactile poking can enhance the grasping success rate significantly from 38.9% to 85.2%. Thanks to its simplicity, our proposed approach could also be adopted by other force or tactile sensors and could be used for grasping of other challenging objects. All the materials used in this paper are available at https://sites.google.com/view/tactilepoking.
summary: Picking up transparent objects is still a challenging task for robots. The visual properties of transparent objects such as reflection and refraction make the current grasping methods that rely on camera sensing fail to detect and localise them. However, humans can handle the transparent object well by first observing its coarse profile and then poking an area of interest to get a fine profile for grasping. Inspired by this, we propose a novel framework of vision-guided tactile poking for transparent objects grasping. In the proposed framework, a segmentation network is first used to predict the horizontal upper regions named as poking regions, where the robot can poke the object to obtain a good tactile reading while leading to minimal disturbance to the object's state. A poke is then performed with a high-resolution GelSight tactile sensor. Given the local profiles improved with the tactile reading, a heuristic grasp is planned for grasping the transparent object. To mitigate the limitations of real-world data collection and labelling for transparent objects, a large-scale realistic synthetic dataset was constructed. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed segmentation network can predict the potential poking region with a high mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.360, and the vision-guided tactile poking can enhance the grasping success rate significantly from 38.9% to 85.2%. Thanks to its simplicity, our proposed approach could also be adopted by other force or tactile sensors and could be used for grasping of other challenging objects. All the materials used in this paper are available at https://sites.google.com/view/tactilepoking.
abstract_zh: 对于机器人来说，拾取透明物体仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。透明物体的反射、折射等视觉特性使得当前依赖相机传感的抓取方法无法检测和定位它们。然而，人类可以通过首先观察透明物体的粗糙轮廓，然后戳感兴趣的区域以获得用于抓握的精细轮廓来很好地处理透明物体。受此启发，我们提出了一种用于透明物体抓取的视觉引导触觉戳的新颖框架。在所提出的框架中，首先使用分割网络来预测称为戳区域的水平上部区域，其中机器人可以戳物体以获得良好的触觉读数，同时对物体状态的干扰最小。然后用高分辨率 GelSight 触觉传感器进行刺戳。鉴于通过触觉读取改进了局部轮廓，计划采用启发式抓握来抓握透明物体。为了减轻现实世界数据收集和透明物体标记的限制，构建了一个大规模的真实合成数据集。大量实验表明，我们提出的分割网络可以以 0.360 的高平均精度 (mAP) 预测潜在的戳戳区域，并且视觉引导的触觉戳戳可以将抓取成功率从 38.9% 显着提高到 85.2%。由于其简单性，我们提出的方法也可以被其他力或触觉传感器采用，并且可以用于抓取其他具有挑战性的物体。本文使用的所有材料均可在 https://sites.google.com/view/tactilepoking 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于视觉引导的触觉探测框架，用于解决机器人抓取透明物体时因反射和折射导致的视觉感知难题。
AI.motivation: 透明物体因光学特性导致传统视觉抓取方法失效，而人类通过观察轮廓并触觉探测关键区域实现抓取，启发了该研究。
AI.method: 首先利用分割网络预测物体上方水平区域作为探测点，通过高分辨率GelSight传感器获取触觉信息，结合局部轮廓规划启发式抓取策略，并构建大规模合成数据集辅助训练。
AI.result: 分割网络达到0.360的mAP，抓取成功率从38.9%提升至85.2%，方法具有通用性可适配其他传感器和复杂物体。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过视觉-触觉协同探测有效解决了透明物体抓取问题，为机器人感知与操作提供了新思路。

conclusion: 该方法通过视觉-触觉协同探测有效解决了透明物体抓取问题，为机器人感知与操作提供了新思路。
search_text: Where Shall I Touch? Vision-Guided Tactile Poking for Transparent Object Grasping 我应该触摸哪里？用于抓取透明物体的视觉引导触觉戳 Picking up transparent objects is still a challenging task for robots. The visual properties of transparent objects such as reflection and refraction make the current grasping methods that rely on camera sensing fail to detect and localise them. However, humans can handle the transparent object well by first observing its coarse profile and then poking an area of interest to get a fine profile for grasping. Inspired by this, we propose a novel framework of vision-guided tactile poking for transparent objects grasping. In the proposed framework, a segmentation network is first used to predict the horizontal upper regions named as poking regions, where the robot can poke the object to obtain a good tactile reading while leading to minimal disturbance to the object's state. A poke is then performed with a high-resolution GelSight tactile sensor. Given the local profiles improved with the tactile reading, a heuristic grasp is planned for grasping the transparent object. To mitigate the limitations of real-world data collection and labelling for transparent objects, a large-scale realistic synthetic dataset was constructed. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed segmentation network can predict the potential poking region with a high mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.360, and the vision-guided tactile poking can enhance the grasping success rate significantly from 38.9% to 85.2%. Thanks to its simplicity, our proposed approach could also be adopted by other force or tactile sensors and could be used for grasping of other challenging objects. All the materials used in this paper are available at https://sites.google.com/view/tactilepoking. 对于机器人来说，拾取透明物体仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。透明物体的反射、折射等视觉特性使得当前依赖相机传感的抓取方法无法检测和定位它们。然而，人类可以通过首先观察透明物体的粗糙轮廓，然后戳感兴趣的区域以获得用于抓握的精细轮廓来很好地处理透明物体。受此启发，我们提出了一种用于透明物体抓取的视觉引导触觉戳的新颖框架。在所提出的框架中，首先使用分割网络来预测称为戳区域的水平上部区域，其中机器人可以戳物体以获得良好的触觉读数，同时对物体状态的干扰最小。然后用高分辨率 GelSight 触觉传感器进行刺戳。鉴于通过触觉读取改进了局部轮廓，计划采用启发式抓握来抓握透明物体。为了减轻现实世界数据收集和透明物体标记的限制，构建了一个大规模的真实合成数据集。大量实验表明，我们提出的分割网络可以以 0.360 的高平均精度 (mAP) 预测潜在的戳戳区域，并且视觉引导的触觉戳戳可以将抓取成功率从 38.9% 显着提高到 85.2%。由于其简单性，我们提出的方法也可以被其他力或触觉传感器采用，并且可以用于抓取其他具有挑战性的物体。本文使用的所有材料均可在 https://sites.google.com/view/tactilepoking 上获取。 本文提出了一种基于视觉引导的触觉探测框架，用于解决机器人抓取透明物体时因反射和折射导致的视觉感知难题。 Jiaqi Jiang,Guanqun Cao,Aaron Butterworth,Thanh-Toan Do,Shan Luo cs.RO

title: A Multi-Head Model for Continual Learning via Out-of-Distribution Replay
title_zh: 通过分布外重放进行持续学习的多头模型
abstract: This paper studies class incremental learning (CIL) of continual learning (CL). Many approaches have been proposed to deal with catastrophic forgetting (CF) in CIL. Most methods incrementally construct a single classifier for all classes of all tasks in a single head network. To prevent CF, a popular approach is to memorize a small number of samples from previous tasks and replay them during training of the new task. However, this approach still suffers from serious CF as the parameters learned for previous tasks are updated or adjusted with only the limited number of saved samples in the memory. This paper proposes an entirely different approach that builds a separate classifier (head) for each task (called a multi-head model) using a transformer network, called MORE. Instead of using the saved samples in memory to update the network for previous tasks/classes in the existing approach, MORE leverages the saved samples to build a task specific classifier (adding a new classification head) without updating the network learned for previous tasks/classes. The model for the new task in MORE is trained to learn the classes of the task and also to detect samples that are not from the same data distribution (i.e., out-of-distribution (OOD)) of the task. This enables the classifier for the task to which the test instance belongs to produce a high score for the correct class and the classifiers of other tasks to produce low scores because the test instance is not from the data distributions of these classifiers. Experimental results show that MORE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines and is also naturally capable of performing OOD detection in the continual learning setting.
summary: This paper studies class incremental learning (CIL) of continual learning (CL). Many approaches have been proposed to deal with catastrophic forgetting (CF) in CIL. Most methods incrementally construct a single classifier for all classes of all tasks in a single head network. To prevent CF, a popular approach is to memorize a small number of samples from previous tasks and replay them during training of the new task. However, this approach still suffers from serious CF as the parameters learned for previous tasks are updated or adjusted with only the limited number of saved samples in the memory. This paper proposes an entirely different approach that builds a separate classifier (head) for each task (called a multi-head model) using a transformer network, called MORE. Instead of using the saved samples in memory to update the network for previous tasks/classes in the existing approach, MORE leverages the saved samples to build a task specific classifier (adding a new classification head) without updating the network learned for previous tasks/classes. The model for the new task in MORE is trained to learn the classes of the task and also to detect samples that are not from the same data distribution (i.e., out-of-distribution (OOD)) of the task. This enables the classifier for the task to which the test instance belongs to produce a high score for the correct class and the classifiers of other tasks to produce low scores because the test instance is not from the data distributions of these classifiers. Experimental results show that MORE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines and is also naturally capable of performing OOD detection in the continual learning setting.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了持续学习（CL）的课堂增量学习（CIL）。人们提出了许多方法来处理 CIL 中的灾难性遗忘（CF）。大多数方法增量地为单头网络中所有任务的所有类构建单个分类器。为了防止 CF，一种流行的方法是记住以前任务中的少量样本，并在新任务的训练过程中重放它们。然而，这种方法仍然存在严重的CF问题，因为之前任务学习到的参数是通过内存中保存的有限数量的样本来更新或调整的。本文提出了一种完全不同的方法，使用称为 MORE 的变压器网络为每个任务构建一个单独的分类器（头）（称为多头模型）。 MORE 不是使用内存中保存的样本来更新现有方法中先前任务/类的网络，而是利用保存的样本来构建特定于任务的分类器（添加新的分类头），而不更新为先前任务/类学习的网络。 MORE 中新任务的模型经过训练，可以学习任务的类别，并检测不是来自任务的同一数据分布（即分布外 (OOD)）的样本。这使得测试实例所属任务的分类器能够为正确的类别产生高分，而其他任务的分类器能够产生低分，因为测试实例不是来自这些分类器的数据分布。实验结果表明，MORE 的性能优于最先进的基线，并且自然能够在持续学习环境中执行 OOD 检测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为MORE的多头模型，通过使用独立分类器和分布外检测解决持续学习中的灾难性遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 传统方法通过回放旧样本更新网络参数，但因样本有限导致参数调整不足，仍存在严重遗忘问题。需探索不更新旧任务参数的新方法。
AI.method: 基于Transformer构建多头模型MORE，为每个任务单独建立分类器（头），利用保存样本构建新分类器而不更新旧网络，并训练模型检测分布外样本。
AI.result: MORE在持续学习任务中优于现有方法，且能自然实现分布外检测，有效缓解灾难性遗忘。
AI.conclusion: 通过解耦任务分类器与分布外检测，MORE为持续学习提供了新思路，验证了多头结构在防止遗忘中的有效性。

search_text: A Multi-Head Model for Continual Learning via Out-of-Distribution Replay 通过分布外重放进行持续学习的多头模型 This paper studies class incremental learning (CIL) of continual learning (CL). Many approaches have been proposed to deal with catastrophic forgetting (CF) in CIL. Most methods incrementally construct a single classifier for all classes of all tasks in a single head network. To prevent CF, a popular approach is to memorize a small number of samples from previous tasks and replay them during training of the new task. However, this approach still suffers from serious CF as the parameters learned for previous tasks are updated or adjusted with only the limited number of saved samples in the memory. This paper proposes an entirely different approach that builds a separate classifier (head) for each task (called a multi-head model) using a transformer network, called MORE. Instead of using the saved samples in memory to update the network for previous tasks/classes in the existing approach, MORE leverages the saved samples to build a task specific classifier (adding a new classification head) without updating the network learned for previous tasks/classes. The model for the new task in MORE is trained to learn the classes of the task and also to detect samples that are not from the same data distribution (i.e., out-of-distribution (OOD)) of the task. This enables the classifier for the task to which the test instance belongs to produce a high score for the correct class and the classifiers of other tasks to produce low scores because the test instance is not from the data distributions of these classifiers. Experimental results show that MORE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines and is also naturally capable of performing OOD detection in the continual learning setting. 本文研究了持续学习（CL）的课堂增量学习（CIL）。人们提出了许多方法来处理 CIL 中的灾难性遗忘（CF）。大多数方法增量地为单头网络中所有任务的所有类构建单个分类器。为了防止 CF，一种流行的方法是记住以前任务中的少量样本，并在新任务的训练过程中重放它们。然而，这种方法仍然存在严重的CF问题，因为之前任务学习到的参数是通过内存中保存的有限数量的样本来更新或调整的。本文提出了一种完全不同的方法，使用称为 MORE 的变压器网络为每个任务构建一个单独的分类器（头）（称为多头模型）。 MORE 不是使用内存中保存的样本来更新现有方法中先前任务/类的网络，而是利用保存的样本来构建特定于任务的分类器（添加新的分类头），而不更新为先前任务/类学习的网络。 MORE 中新任务的模型经过训练，可以学习任务的类别，并检测不是来自任务的同一数据分布（即分布外 (OOD)）的样本。这使得测试实例所属任务的分类器能够为正确的类别产生高分，而其他任务的分类器能够产生低分，因为测试实例不是来自这些分类器的数据分布。实验结果表明，MORE 的性能优于最先进的基线，并且自然能够在持续学习环境中执行 OOD 检测。 本文提出一种名为MORE的多头模型，通过使用独立分类器和分布外检测解决持续学习中的灾难性遗忘问题。 Gyuhak Kim,Zixuan Ke,Bing Liu cs.LG,cs.CV

title: Solving systems of linear equations through zero forcing set and application to lights out
title_zh: 通过迫零集求解线性方程组并应用于熄灯
abstract: Let $\mathbb{F}$ be any field, we consider solving $Ax=b$ repeatedly for a matrix $A\in\mathbb{F}^{n\times n}$ of $m$ non-zero elements, and multiple different $b\in\mathbb{F}^{n}$. If we are given a zero forcing set of $A$ of size $k$, we can then build a data structure in $O(mk)$ time, such that each instance of $Ax=b$ can be solved in $O(k^2+m)$ time. As an application, we show how the lights out game in an $n\times n$ grid is solved in $O(n^3)$ time, and then improve the running time to $O(n^ω\log n)$ by exploiting the repeated structure in grids.
summary: Let $\mathbb{F}$ be any field, we consider solving $Ax=b$ repeatedly for a matrix $A\in\mathbb{F}^{n\times n}$ of $m$ non-zero elements, and multiple different $b\in\mathbb{F}^{n}$. If we are given a zero forcing set of $A$ of size $k$, we can then build a data structure in $O(mk)$ time, such that each instance of $Ax=b$ can be solved in $O(k^2+m)$ time. As an application, we show how the lights out game in an $n\times n$ grid is solved in $O(n^3)$ time, and then improve the running time to $O(n^ω\log n)$ by exploiting the repeated structure in grids.
abstract_zh: 令$\mathbb{F}$为任意域，我们考虑对$m$个非零元素的矩阵$A\in\mathbb{F}^{n\times n}$以及多个不同的$b\in\mathbb{F}^{n}$重复求解$Ax=b$。如果给定一个大小为 $k$ 的 $A$ 的迫零集，我们就可以在 $O(mk)$ 时间内构建一个数据结构，这样 $Ax=b$ 的每个实例都可以在 $O(k^2+m)$ 时间内求解。作为一个应用程序，我们展示了如何在 $O(n^3)$ 时间内解决 $n\times n$ 网格中的熄灯游戏，然后通过利用网格中的重复结构将运行时间提高到 $O(n^ω\log n)$。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何通过零强制集高效求解线性方程组，并将其应用于关灯游戏问题。
AI.motivation: 重复求解线性方程组在多个领域具有重要应用，而零强制集提供了一种潜在的高效解决方案，尤其适用于结构化问题如关灯游戏。
AI.method: 利用零强制集构建数据结构，将方程组求解时间复杂度降低至O(k² + m)，并通过网格结构优化关灯游戏的求解效率。
AI.result: 关灯游戏的求解时间从O(n³)优化至O(n^ω log n)，展示了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出了一种基于零强制集的高效线性方程组求解方法，并成功应用于关灯游戏，验证了其实际价值。

title: Cognitive Modeling of Semantic Fluency Using Transformers
title_zh: 使用 Transformers 进行语义流畅性认知建模
comment: Cognitive Aspects of Knowledge Representation workshop at IJCAI-ECAI 2022
comments: Cognitive Aspects of Knowledge Representation workshop at IJCAI-ECAI 2022
abstract: Can deep language models be explanatory models of human cognition? If so, what are their limits? In order to explore this question, we propose an approach called hyperparameter hypothesization that uses predictive hyperparameter tuning in order to find individuating descriptors of cognitive-behavioral profiles. We take the first step in this approach by predicting human performance in the semantic fluency task (SFT), a well-studied task in cognitive science that has never before been modeled using transformer-based language models (TLMs). In our task setup, we compare several approaches to predicting which word an individual performing SFT will utter next. We report preliminary evidence suggesting that, despite obvious implementational differences in how people and TLMs learn and use language, TLMs can be used to identify individual differences in human fluency task behaviors better than existing computational models, and may offer insights into human memory retrieval strategies -- cognitive process not typically considered to be the kinds of things TLMs can model. Finally, we discuss the implications of this work for cognitive modeling of knowledge representations.
summary: Can deep language models be explanatory models of human cognition? If so, what are their limits? In order to explore this question, we propose an approach called hyperparameter hypothesization that uses predictive hyperparameter tuning in order to find individuating descriptors of cognitive-behavioral profiles. We take the first step in this approach by predicting human performance in the semantic fluency task (SFT), a well-studied task in cognitive science that has never before been modeled using transformer-based language models (TLMs). In our task setup, we compare several approaches to predicting which word an individual performing SFT will utter next. We report preliminary evidence suggesting that, despite obvious implementational differences in how people and TLMs learn and use language, TLMs can be used to identify individual differences in human fluency task behaviors better than existing computational models, and may offer insights into human memory retrieval strategies -- cognitive process not typically considered to be the kinds of things TLMs can model. Finally, we discuss the implications of this work for cognitive modeling of knowledge representations.
abstract_zh: 深层语言模型可以成为人类认知的解释模型吗？如果是这样，他们的限制是什么？为了探索这个问题，我们提出了一种称为超参数假设的方法，该方法使用预测性超参数调整来找到认知行为概况的个性化描述符。我们在这种方法中迈出了第一步，预测人类在语义流畅性任务（SFT）中的表现，这是认知科学中经过充分研究的任务，之前从未使用基于变压器的语言模型（TLM）进行建模。在我们的任务设置中，我们比较了几种预测执行 SFT 的人接下来会说出哪个单词的方法。我们报告的初步证据表明，尽管人们和 TLM 学习和使用语言的方式存在明显的实施差异，但 TLM 可以比现有的计算模型更好地识别人类流畅性任务行为的个体差异，并且可以提供对人类记忆检索策略的见解——认知过程通常不被认为是 TLM 可以建模的类型。最后，我们讨论这项工作对知识表示的认知建模的影响。

AI.tldr: 本论文探讨使用Transformer模型对人类语义流畅性任务的认知行为进行建模，分析其作为认知解释模型的潜力与局限性。
AI.motivation: 研究深度语言模型是否能作为人类认知的解释模型，并探索其在语义流畅性任务中的应用潜力，填补现有计算模型的不足。
AI.method: 提出超参数假设方法，通过预测性超参数调优识别认知行为特征，并比较不同模型预测人类在语义流畅性任务中的下一个词的性能。
AI.result: 初步证据表明，Transformer模型能更有效地识别人类流畅性任务中的个体差异，并可能揭示人类记忆检索策略等传统认为无法建模的认知过程。
AI.conclusion: Transformer模型在认知建模中展现出潜力，为理解人类知识表征和记忆机制提供了新视角，同时需进一步探索其局限性。

search_text: Cognitive Modeling of Semantic Fluency Using Transformers 使用 Transformers 进行语义流畅性认知建模 Can deep language models be explanatory models of human cognition? If so, what are their limits? In order to explore this question, we propose an approach called hyperparameter hypothesization that uses predictive hyperparameter tuning in order to find individuating descriptors of cognitive-behavioral profiles. We take the first step in this approach by predicting human performance in the semantic fluency task (SFT), a well-studied task in cognitive science that has never before been modeled using transformer-based language models (TLMs). In our task setup, we compare several approaches to predicting which word an individual performing SFT will utter next. We report preliminary evidence suggesting that, despite obvious implementational differences in how people and TLMs learn and use language, TLMs can be used to identify individual differences in human fluency task behaviors better than existing computational models, and may offer insights into human memory retrieval strategies -- cognitive process not typically considered to be the kinds of things TLMs can model. Finally, we discuss the implications of this work for cognitive modeling of knowledge representations. 深层语言模型可以成为人类认知的解释模型吗？如果是这样，他们的限制是什么？为了探索这个问题，我们提出了一种称为超参数假设的方法，该方法使用预测性超参数调整来找到认知行为概况的个性化描述符。我们在这种方法中迈出了第一步，预测人类在语义流畅性任务（SFT）中的表现，这是认知科学中经过充分研究的任务，之前从未使用基于变压器的语言模型（TLM）进行建模。在我们的任务设置中，我们比较了几种预测执行 SFT 的人接下来会说出哪个单词的方法。我们报告的初步证据表明，尽管人们和 TLM 学习和使用语言的方式存在明显的实施差异，但 TLM 可以比现有的计算模型更好地识别人类流畅性任务行为的个体差异，并且可以提供对人类记忆检索策略的见解——认知过程通常不被认为是 TLM 可以建模的类型。最后，我们讨论这项工作对知识表示的认知建模的影响。 本论文探讨使用Transformer模型对人类语义流畅性任务的认知行为进行建模，分析其作为认知解释模型的潜力与局限性。 Animesh Nighojkar,Anna Khlyzova,John Licato cs.CL,cs.AI

title: Learning Primitive-aware Discriminative Representations for Few-shot Learning
title_zh: 学习用于小样本学习的基元感知判别表示
abstract: Few-shot learning (FSL) aims to learn a classifier that can be easily adapted to recognize novel classes with only a few labeled examples. Some recent work about FSL has yielded promising classification performance, where the image-level feature is used to calculate the similarity among samples for classification. However, the image-level feature ignores abundant fine-grained and structural in-formation of objects that may be transferable and consistent between seen and unseen classes. How can humans easily identify novel classes with several sam-ples? Some study from cognitive science argues that humans can recognize novel categories through primitives. Although base and novel categories are non-overlapping, they can share some primitives in common. Inspired by above re-search, we propose a Primitive Mining and Reasoning Network (PMRN) to learn primitive-aware representations based on metric-based FSL model. Concretely, we first add Self-supervision Jigsaw task (SSJ) for feature extractor parallelly, guiding the model to encode visual pattern corresponding to object parts into fea-ture channels. To further mine discriminative representations, an Adaptive Chan-nel Grouping (ACG) method is applied to cluster and weight spatially and se-mantically related visual patterns to generate a group of visual primitives. To fur-ther enhance the discriminability and transferability of primitives, we propose a visual primitive Correlation Reasoning Network (CRN) based on graph convolu-tional network to learn abundant structural information and internal correlation among primitives. Finally, a primitive-level metric is conducted for classification in a meta-task based on episodic training strategy. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art results on six standard benchmarks.
summary: Few-shot learning (FSL) aims to learn a classifier that can be easily adapted to recognize novel classes with only a few labeled examples. Some recent work about FSL has yielded promising classification performance, where the image-level feature is used to calculate the similarity among samples for classification. However, the image-level feature ignores abundant fine-grained and structural in-formation of objects that may be transferable and consistent between seen and unseen classes. How can humans easily identify novel classes with several sam-ples? Some study from cognitive science argues that humans can recognize novel categories through primitives. Although base and novel categories are non-overlapping, they can share some primitives in common. Inspired by above re-search, we propose a Primitive Mining and Reasoning Network (PMRN) to learn primitive-aware representations based on metric-based FSL model. Concretely, we first add Self-supervision Jigsaw task (SSJ) for feature extractor parallelly, guiding the model to encode visual pattern corresponding to object parts into fea-ture channels. To further mine discriminative representations, an Adaptive Chan-nel Grouping (ACG) method is applied to cluster and weight spatially and se-mantically related visual patterns to generate a group of visual primitives. To fur-ther enhance the discriminability and transferability of primitives, we propose a visual primitive Correlation Reasoning Network (CRN) based on graph convolu-tional network to learn abundant structural information and internal correlation among primitives. Finally, a primitive-level metric is conducted for classification in a meta-task based on episodic training strategy. Extensive experiments show that our method achieves state-of-the-art results on six standard benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 少样本学习（FSL）旨在学习一种分类器，该分类器可以轻松适应仅通过少量标记示例即可识别新类别。最近一些关于 FSL 的工作已经取得了有前途的分类性能，其中图像级特征用于计算样本之间的相似性以进行分类。然而，图像级特征忽略了对象的丰富的细粒度和结构信息，这些信息可以在可见类和不可见类之间转移和一致。人类如何通过多个样本轻松识别新类别？认知科学的一些研究认为，人类可以通过基元识别新类别。尽管基本类别和新类别不重叠，但它们可以共享一些共同的基元。受上述研究的启发，我们提出了一种原始挖掘和推理网络（PMRN）来学习基于基于度量的 FSL 模型的原始感知表示。具体来说，我们首先并行地为特征提取器添加自监督拼图任务（SSJ），引导模型将与对象部分相对应的视觉模式编码到特征通道中。为了进一步挖掘判别性表示，应用自适应通道分组（ACG）方法对空间和语义相关的视觉模式进行聚类和加权，以生成一组视觉基元。为了进一步增强图元的可区分性和可迁移性，我们提出了一种基于图卷积网络的视觉图元相关推理网络（CRN），以学习图元之间丰富的结构信息和内部关联性。最后，基于情景训练策略对元任务中的分类进行原始级度量。大量的实验表明，我们的方法在六个标准基准上取得了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于原始感知判别表示的少样本学习方法，通过挖掘视觉原语提升模型对新类别的识别能力。
AI.motivation: 现有少样本学习方法依赖图像级特征计算相似度，但忽略了物体的细粒度和结构信息。人类通过共享的原始部件识别新类别，启发模型应关注可迁移的原语信息。
AI.method: 设计Primitive Mining and Reasoning Network（PMRN），包含自监督拼图任务提取部件特征、自适应通道分组挖掘原语、图卷积网络建模原语关联，并采用原语级度量进行分类。
AI.result: 在六个标准基准测试中取得当时最先进的少样本学习性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过建模视觉原语的结构关联和语义一致性，有效提升了少样本学习的判别能力和知识迁移效果。

title: Wolfies at SemEval-2022 Task 8: Feature extraction pipeline with transformers for Multi-lingual news article similarity
title_zh: Wolfies 参加 SemEval-2022 任务 8：使用转换器进行特征提取管道以实现多语言新闻文章相似性
abstract: This work is about finding the similarity between a pair of news articles. There are seven different objective similarity metrics provided in the dataset for each pair and the news articles are in multiple different languages. On top of the pre-trained embedding model, we calculated cosine similarity for baseline results and feed-forward neural network was then trained on top of it to improve the results. We also built separate pipelines for each similarity metric for feature extraction. We could see significant improvement from baseline results using feature extraction and feed-forward neural network.
summary: This work is about finding the similarity between a pair of news articles. There are seven different objective similarity metrics provided in the dataset for each pair and the news articles are in multiple different languages. On top of the pre-trained embedding model, we calculated cosine similarity for baseline results and feed-forward neural network was then trained on top of it to improve the results. We also built separate pipelines for each similarity metric for feature extraction. We could see significant improvement from baseline results using feature extraction and feed-forward neural network.
abstract_zh: 这项工作是关于寻找一对新闻文章之间的相似性。数据集中为每一对提供了七种不同的客观相似性度量，并且新闻文章采用多种不同的语言。在预先训练的嵌入模型之上，我们计算了基线结果的余弦相似度，然后在其之上训练前馈神经网络以改进结果。我们还为每个相似性度量构建了单独的管道以进行特征提取。我们可以看到使用特征提取和前馈神经网络的基线结果有显着改善。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过基于transformers的特征提取管道，解决多语言新闻文章相似度判断问题。
AI.motivation: 多语言新闻文章相似度判断面临语言差异和特征提取挑战，需改进基线方法以提升效果。
AI.method: 基于预训练嵌入模型计算余弦相似度作为基线，训练前馈神经网络优化结果，并为每个相似度指标构建独立特征提取流程。
AI.result: 特征提取与前馈神经网络结合后，显著优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 特征工程与神经网络结合可有效提升多语言新闻相似度判断，独立处理不同指标有助于性能优化。

search_text: Wolfies at SemEval-2022 Task 8: Feature extraction pipeline with transformers for Multi-lingual news article similarity Wolfies 参加 SemEval-2022 任务 8：使用转换器进行特征提取管道以实现多语言新闻文章相似性 This work is about finding the similarity between a pair of news articles. There are seven different objective similarity metrics provided in the dataset for each pair and the news articles are in multiple different languages. On top of the pre-trained embedding model, we calculated cosine similarity for baseline results and feed-forward neural network was then trained on top of it to improve the results. We also built separate pipelines for each similarity metric for feature extraction. We could see significant improvement from baseline results using feature extraction and feed-forward neural network. 这项工作是关于寻找一对新闻文章之间的相似性。数据集中为每一对提供了七种不同的客观相似性度量，并且新闻文章采用多种不同的语言。在预先训练的嵌入模型之上，我们计算了基线结果的余弦相似度，然后在其之上训练前馈神经网络以改进结果。我们还为每个相似性度量构建了单独的管道以进行特征提取。我们可以看到使用特征提取和前馈神经网络的基线结果有显着改善。 本文旨在通过基于transformers的特征提取管道，解决多语言新闻文章相似度判断问题。 Nikhil Goel,Ranjith Reddy cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Xinlin Li,Bang Liu,Rui Heng Yang,Vanessa Courville,Chao Xing,Vahid Partovi Nia
title: DenseShift: Towards Accurate and Efficient Low-Bit Power-of-Two Quantization
title_zh: DenseShift：迈向准确高效的低位二次幂量化
abstract: Efficiently deploying deep neural networks on low-resource edge devices is challenging due to their ever-increasing resource requirements. To address this issue, researchers have proposed multiplication-free neural networks, such as Power-of-Two quantization, or also known as Shift networks, which aim to reduce memory usage and simplify computation. However, existing low-bit Shift networks are not as accurate as their full-precision counterparts, typically suffering from limited weight range encoding schemes and quantization loss. In this paper, we propose the DenseShift network, which significantly improves the accuracy of Shift networks, achieving competitive performance to full-precision networks for vision and speech applications. In addition, we introduce a method to deploy an efficient DenseShift network using non-quantized floating-point activations, while obtaining 1.6X speed-up over existing methods. To achieve this, we demonstrate that zero-weight values in low-bit Shift networks do not contribute to model capacity and negatively impact inference computation. To address this issue, we propose a zero-free shifting mechanism that simplifies inference and increases model capacity. We further propose a sign-scale decomposition design to enhance training efficiency and a low-variance random initialization strategy to improve the model's transfer learning performance. Our extensive experiments on various computer vision and speech tasks demonstrate that DenseShift outperforms existing low-bit multiplication-free networks and achieves competitive performance compared to full-precision networks. Furthermore, our proposed approach exhibits strong transfer learning performance without a drop in accuracy. Our code was released on GitHub.
summary: Efficiently deploying deep neural networks on low-resource edge devices is challenging due to their ever-increasing resource requirements. To address this issue, researchers have proposed multiplication-free neural networks, such as Power-of-Two quantization, or also known as Shift networks, which aim to reduce memory usage and simplify computation. However, existing low-bit Shift networks are not as accurate as their full-precision counterparts, typically suffering from limited weight range encoding schemes and quantization loss. In this paper, we propose the DenseShift network, which significantly improves the accuracy of Shift networks, achieving competitive performance to full-precision networks for vision and speech applications. In addition, we introduce a method to deploy an efficient DenseShift network using non-quantized floating-point activations, while obtaining 1.6X speed-up over existing methods. To achieve this, we demonstrate that zero-weight values in low-bit Shift networks do not contribute to model capacity and negatively impact inference computation. To address this issue, we propose a zero-free shifting mechanism that simplifies inference and increases model capacity. We further propose a sign-scale decomposition design to enhance training efficiency and a low-variance random initialization strategy to improve the model's transfer learning performance. Our extensive experiments on various computer vision and speech tasks demonstrate that DenseShift outperforms existing low-bit multiplication-free networks and achieves competitive performance compared to full-precision networks. Furthermore, our proposed approach exhibits strong transfer learning performance without a drop in accuracy. Our code was released on GitHub.
abstract_zh: 由于资源需求不断增加，在资源匮乏的边缘设备上有效部署深度神经网络具有挑战性。为了解决这个问题，研究人员提出了无乘法神经网络，例如二次幂量化，也称为移位网络，其目的是减少内存使用并简化计算。然而，现有的低比特移位网络不如全精度网络准确，通常受到有限权重范围编码方案和量化损失的影响。在本文中，我们提出了 DenseShift 网络，它显着提高了 Shift 网络的准确性，在视觉和语音应用中实现了与全精度网络竞争的性能。此外，我们引入了一种使用非量化浮点激活来部署高效 DenseShift 网络的方法，同时获得比现有方法 1.6 倍的加速。为了实现这一目标，我们证明低位移位网络中的零权重值不会对模型容量做出贡献，也不会对推理计算产生负面影响。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种零自由移位机制，可以简化推理并提高模型容量。我们进一步提出了一种符号尺度分解设计来提高训练效率，并提出一种低方差随机初始化策略来提高模型的迁移学习性能。我们对各种计算机视觉和语音任务进行的广泛实验表明，DenseShift 优于现有的低位无乘法网络，并与全精度网络相比实现了具有竞争力的性能。此外，我们提出的方法表现出强大的迁移学习性能，而不会降低准确性。我们的代码已发布在 GitHub 上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DenseShift网络，通过改进低比特Power-of-Two量化方法，在保持模型精度的同时提升计算效率，适用于边缘设备部署。
AI.motivation: 现有低比特Shift网络存在精度不足、量化损失大及计算效率低的问题，限制了其在边缘设备上的应用。
AI.method: 提出零值消除的位移机制、符号-尺度分解设计及低方差初始化策略，优化量化过程并提升模型容量与训练效率。
AI.result: 在多个视觉与语音任务中，DenseShift相较现有低比特网络精度提升显著，且推理速度加快1.6倍，迁移学习性能无下降。
AI.conclusion: DenseShift通过创新量化机制与优化策略，在模型精度、计算效率及迁移能力上达到全精度网络水平，适用于资源受限场景。

title: SnowFormer: Context Interaction Transformer with Scale-awareness for Single Image Desnowing
title_zh: SnowFormer：具有尺度感知能力的上下文交互变压器，用于单图像除雪
abstract: Due to various and complicated snow degradations, single image desnowing is a challenging image restoration task. As prior arts can not handle it ideally, we propose a novel transformer, SnowFormer, which explores efficient cross-attentions to build local-global context interaction across patches and surpasses existing works that employ local operators or vanilla transformers. Compared to prior desnowing methods and universal image restoration methods, SnowFormer has several benefits. Firstly, unlike the multi-head self-attention in recent image restoration Vision Transformers, SnowFormer incorporates the multi-head cross-attention mechanism to perform local-global context interaction between scale-aware snow queries and local-patch embeddings. Second, the snow queries in SnowFormer are generated by the query generator from aggregated scale-aware features, which are rich in potential clean cues, leading to superior restoration results. Third, SnowFormer outshines advanced state-of-the-art desnowing networks and the prevalent universal image restoration transformers on six synthetic and real-world datasets. The code is released in \url{https://github.com/Ephemeral182/SnowFormer}.
summary: Due to various and complicated snow degradations, single image desnowing is a challenging image restoration task. As prior arts can not handle it ideally, we propose a novel transformer, SnowFormer, which explores efficient cross-attentions to build local-global context interaction across patches and surpasses existing works that employ local operators or vanilla transformers. Compared to prior desnowing methods and universal image restoration methods, SnowFormer has several benefits. Firstly, unlike the multi-head self-attention in recent image restoration Vision Transformers, SnowFormer incorporates the multi-head cross-attention mechanism to perform local-global context interaction between scale-aware snow queries and local-patch embeddings. Second, the snow queries in SnowFormer are generated by the query generator from aggregated scale-aware features, which are rich in potential clean cues, leading to superior restoration results. Third, SnowFormer outshines advanced state-of-the-art desnowing networks and the prevalent universal image restoration transformers on six synthetic and real-world datasets. The code is released in \url{https://github.com/Ephemeral182/SnowFormer}.
abstract_zh: 由于雪质退化情况多样且复杂，单幅图像除雪是一项具有挑战性的图像恢复任务。由于现有技术无法理想地处理它，我们提出了一种新颖的变压器，SnowFormer，它探索有效的交叉注意力来构建跨补丁的本地-全局上下文交互，并超越了使用本地运算符或普通变压器的现有作品。与现有的除雪方法和通用图像恢复方法相比，SnowFormer 有几个优点。首先，与最近图像恢复 Vision Transformer 中的多头自注意力不同，SnowFormer 结合了多头交叉注意力机制，在尺度感知雪查询和局部补丁嵌入之间执行局部-全局上下文交互。其次，SnowFormer 中的雪查询是由查询生成器根据聚合的尺度感知特征生成的，这些特征富含潜在的干净线索，从而产生出色的恢复结果。第三，SnowFormer 在六个合成和真实数据集上超越了先进的最先进的除雪网络和流行的通用图像恢复变压器。代码发布在 \url{https://github.com/Ephemeral182/SnowFormer} 中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为SnowFormer的新型Transformer模型，用于解决单图像去雪任务，通过高效的跨注意力机制实现局部与全局上下文交互。
AI.motivation: 现有去雪方法难以处理复杂多样的雪退化问题，传统局部算子和普通Transformer存在上下文建模不足的缺陷，需探索更有效的全局-局部交互机制。
AI.method: 设计多头交叉注意力机制，通过尺度感知的雪查询与局部块嵌入进行上下文交互；利用聚合的尺度感知特征生成雪查询，增强清洁线索提取能力。
AI.result: 在六个合成与真实数据集上超越先进去雪网络和通用图像修复Transformer，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: SnowFormer通过创新的跨注意力机制和尺度感知设计，在单图像去雪任务中展现出优越的性能，为复杂退化场景提供了有效解决方案。

Second, the snow queries in SnowFormer are generated by the query generator from aggregated scale-aware features, which are rich in potential clean cues, leading to superior restoration results. Third, SnowFormer outshines advanced state-of-the-art desnowing networks and the prevalent universal image restoration transformers on six synthetic and real-world datasets. The code is released in \url{https://github.com/Ephemeral182/SnowFormer}. 由于雪质退化情况多样且复杂，单幅图像除雪是一项具有挑战性的图像恢复任务。由于现有技术无法理想地处理它，我们提出了一种新颖的变压器，SnowFormer，它探索有效的交叉注意力来构建跨补丁的本地-全局上下文交互，并超越了使用本地运算符或普通变压器的现有作品。与现有的除雪方法和通用图像恢复方法相比，SnowFormer 有几个优点。首先，与最近图像恢复 Vision Transformer 中的多头自注意力不同，SnowFormer 结合了多头交叉注意力机制，在尺度感知雪查询和局部补丁嵌入之间执行局部-全局上下文交互。其次，SnowFormer 中的雪查询是由查询生成器根据聚合的尺度感知特征生成的，这些特征富含潜在的干净线索，从而产生出色的恢复结果。第三，SnowFormer 在六个合成和真实数据集上超越了先进的最先进的除雪网络和流行的通用图像恢复变压器。代码发布在 \url{https://github.com/Ephemeral182/SnowFormer} 中。 本文提出一种名为SnowFormer的新型Transformer模型，用于解决单图像去雪任务，通过高效的跨注意力机制实现局部与全局上下文交互。 Sixiang Chen,Tian Ye,Yun Liu,Erkang Chen cs.CV

authors_text: Tahsin Aziz,Tashreef Muhammad,Md. Rashedul Karim Chowdhury,Mohammad Shafiul Alam
title: An Approach of Adjusting the Switch Probability based on Dimension Size: A Case Study for Performance Improvement of the Flower Pollination Algorithm
title_zh: 基于维度大小调整切换概率的方法——以花授粉算法性能改进为例
comment: 9 pages, 2 figures, 3 tables
comments: 9 pages, 2 figures, 3 tables
abstract: Numerous meta-heuristic algorithms have been influenced by nature. Over the past couple of decades, their quantity has been significantly escalating. The majority of these algorithms attempt to emulate natural biological and physical phenomena. This research concentrates on the Flower Pollination algorithm, which is one of several bio-inspired algorithms. The original approach was suggested for pollen grain exploration and exploitation in confined space using a specific global pollination and local pollination strategy. As a "swarm intelligence" meta-heuristic algorithm, its strength lies in locating the vicinity of the optimum solution rather than identifying the minimum. A modification to the original method is detailed in this work. This research found that by changing the specific value of "switch probability" with dynamic values of different dimension sizes and functions, the outcome was mainly improved over the original flower pollination method.
summary: Numerous meta-heuristic algorithms have been influenced by nature. Over the past couple of decades, their quantity has been significantly escalating. The majority of these algorithms attempt to emulate natural biological and physical phenomena. This research concentrates on the Flower Pollination algorithm, which is one of several bio-inspired algorithms. The original approach was suggested for pollen grain exploration and exploitation in confined space using a specific global pollination and local pollination strategy. As a "swarm intelligence" meta-heuristic algorithm, its strength lies in locating the vicinity of the optimum solution rather than identifying the minimum. A modification to the original method is detailed in this work. This research found that by changing the specific value of "switch probability" with dynamic values of different dimension sizes and functions, the outcome was mainly improved over the original flower pollination method.
abstract_zh: 许多元启发式算法都受到自然的影响。在过去的几十年里，它们的数量急剧增加。这些算法中的大多数都试图模拟自然的生物和物理现象。这项研究集中在花授粉算法上，它是几种仿生算法之一。最初的方法是使用特定的全局授粉和局部授粉策略在有限空间内探索和利用花粉粒。作为一种“群体智能”元启发式算法，其优势在于定位最优解的附近而不是识别最小值。这项工作详细介绍了对原始方法的修改。研究发现，通过用不同维度大小和函数的动态值来改变“切换概率”的具体值，结果主要比原来的花授粉方法有所改善。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于维度大小动态调整切换概率的方法，以提升花粉授粉算法（FPA）的性能。
AI.motivation: 原始FPA在寻找最优解附近表现良好，但难以精确确定最小值，需通过改进切换概率机制增强算法适应性。
AI.method: 通过动态调整切换概率的数值，结合不同维度大小和目标函数特性，优化FPA的全局与局部搜索平衡。
AI.result: 实验表明，调整后的算法在多种测试函数和维度下性能优于原始FPA，收敛速度与精度显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 动态切换概率机制有效增强了FPA的优化能力，为生物启发式算法的参数自适应调整提供新思路。

title: Fuse and Attend: Generalized Embedding Learning for Art and Sketches
title_zh: 融合并参与：艺术和素描的广义嵌入学习
comment: Accepted in European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV) 2022 Workshops: DIRA
comments: Accepted in European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV) 2022 Workshops: DIRA
abstract: While deep Embedding Learning approaches have witnessed widespread success in multiple computer vision tasks, the state-of-the-art methods for representing natural images need not necessarily perform well on images from other domains, such as paintings, cartoons, and sketch. This is because of the huge shift in the distribution of data from across these domains, as compared to natural images. Domains like sketch often contain sparse informative pixels. However, recognizing objects in such domains is crucial, given multiple relevant applications leveraging such data, for instance, sketch to image retrieval. Thus, achieving an Embedding Learning model that could perform well across multiple domains is not only challenging, but plays a pivotal role in computer vision. To this end, in this paper, we propose a novel Embedding Learning approach with the goal of generalizing across different domains. During training, given a query image from a domain, we employ gated fusion and attention to generate a positive example, which carries a broad notion of the semantics of the query object category (from across multiple domains). By virtue of Contrastive Learning, we pull the embeddings of the query and positive, in order to learn a representation which is robust across domains. At the same time, to teach the model to be discriminative against examples from different semantic categories (across domains), we also maintain a pool of negative embeddings (from different categories). We show the prowess of our method using the DomainBed framework, on the popular PACS (Photo, Art painting, Cartoon, and Sketch) dataset.
summary: While deep Embedding Learning approaches have witnessed widespread success in multiple computer vision tasks, the state-of-the-art methods for representing natural images need not necessarily perform well on images from other domains, such as paintings, cartoons, and sketch. This is because of the huge shift in the distribution of data from across these domains, as compared to natural images. Domains like sketch often contain sparse informative pixels. However, recognizing objects in such domains is crucial, given multiple relevant applications leveraging such data, for instance, sketch to image retrieval. Thus, achieving an Embedding Learning model that could perform well across multiple domains is not only challenging, but plays a pivotal role in computer vision. To this end, in this paper, we propose a novel Embedding Learning approach with the goal of generalizing across different domains. During training, given a query image from a domain, we employ gated fusion and attention to generate a positive example, which carries a broad notion of the semantics of the query object category (from across multiple domains). By virtue of Contrastive Learning, we pull the embeddings of the query and positive, in order to learn a representation which is robust across domains. At the same time, to teach the model to be discriminative against examples from different semantic categories (across domains), we also maintain a pool of negative embeddings (from different categories). We show the prowess of our method using the DomainBed framework, on the popular PACS (Photo, Art painting, Cartoon, and Sketch) dataset.
abstract_zh: 虽然深度嵌入学习方法在多种计算机视觉任务中取得了广泛的成功，但表示自然图像的最先进方法不一定在其他领域的图像（例如绘画、卡通和素描）上表现良好。这是因为与自然图像相比，这些领域的数据分布发生了巨大变化。像草图这样的领域通常包含稀疏的信息像素。然而，考虑到多个相关应用程序利用此类数据（例如草图到图像检索），识别此类领域中的对象至关重要。因此，实现一个可以在多个领域表现良好的嵌入学习模型不仅具有挑战性，而且在计算机视觉中发挥着关键作用。为此，在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的嵌入学习方法，其目标是跨不同领域进行推广。在训练过程中，给定来自某个域的查询图像，我们采用门控融合和注意力来生成一个正例，该示例承载了查询对象类别（来自多个域）的语义的广泛概念。借助对比学习，我们提取查询和肯定的嵌入，以便学习跨领域稳健的表示。同时，为了教会模型区分来自不同语义类别（跨领域）的示例，我们还维护一个负嵌入池（来自不同类别）。我们使用 DomainBed 框架在流行的 PACS（照片、艺术绘画、卡通和素描）数据集上展示了我们方法的强大功能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种跨领域嵌入学习方法，旨在解决艺术作品和素描等非自然图像领域的表示学习问题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度嵌入学习方法在自然图像领域表现优异，但难以泛化到绘画、卡通、素描等数据分布差异大的领域，而这些领域的物体识别对实际应用至关重要。
AI.method: 通过门控融合与注意力机制生成跨领域语义正样本，结合对比学习拉近查询样本与正样本的嵌入距离，同时利用负样本池增强跨类别判别能力。
AI.result: 在PACS数据集上验证了方法有效性，证明其在跨领域嵌入学习任务中的优越性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过融合多领域语义信息与对比学习机制，有效提升了嵌入表示的跨领域鲁棒性，为多领域视觉任务提供了新思路。

title: Effectiveness of Function Matching in Driving Scene Recognition
title_zh: 功能匹配在驾驶场景识别中的有效性
comment: Autonomous Vehicle Vision (AVVision) Workshop at ECCV2022
comments: Autonomous Vehicle Vision (AVVision) Workshop at ECCV2022
abstract: Knowledge distillation is an effective approach for training compact recognizers required in autonomous driving. Recent studies on image classification have shown that matching student and teacher on a wide range of data points is critical for improving performance in distillation. This concept (called function matching) is suitable for driving scene recognition, where generally an almost infinite amount of unlabeled data are available. In this study, we experimentally investigate the impact of using such a large amount of unlabeled data for distillation on the performance of student models in structured prediction tasks for autonomous driving. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate that the performance of the compact student model can be improved dramatically and even match the performance of the large-scale teacher by knowledge distillation with massive unlabeled data.
summary: Knowledge distillation is an effective approach for training compact recognizers required in autonomous driving. Recent studies on image classification have shown that matching student and teacher on a wide range of data points is critical for improving performance in distillation. This concept (called function matching) is suitable for driving scene recognition, where generally an almost infinite amount of unlabeled data are available. In this study, we experimentally investigate the impact of using such a large amount of unlabeled data for distillation on the performance of student models in structured prediction tasks for autonomous driving. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate that the performance of the compact student model can be improved dramatically and even match the performance of the large-scale teacher by knowledge distillation with massive unlabeled data.
abstract_zh: 知识蒸馏是训练自动驾驶所需的紧凑型识别器的有效方法。最近关于图像分类的研究表明，在广泛的数据点上匹配学生和教师对于提高蒸馏性能至关重要。这个概念（称为功能匹配）适用于驾驶场景识别，其中通常有几乎无限量的未标记数据可用。在本研究中，我们通过实验研究了使用如此大量的未标记数据进行蒸馏对自动驾驶结构化预测任务中的学生模型性能的影响。通过大量的实验，我们证明，通过大量未标记数据的知识蒸馏，紧凑学生模型的性能可以显着提高，甚至可以与大规模教师的性能相匹配。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在自动驾驶场景识别中，通过知识蒸馏结合大量未标记数据进行函数匹配，以提升紧凑型学生模型性能的方法。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶需要高效的紧凑模型，但标注数据有限。函数匹配（Function Matching）在图像分类中已被证明有效，而驾驶场景存在大量未标记数据，因此探索其在该领域的应用具有重要意义。
AI.method: 采用知识蒸馏框架，利用未标记数据对齐教师模型与学生模型的函数特性，通过实验验证在结构化预测任务中使用大规模未标记数据的蒸馏效果。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用大量未标记数据进行蒸馏后，紧凑型学生模型性能显著提升，甚至可接近大型教师模型的水平。
AI.conclusion: 函数匹配结合知识蒸馏能有效利用未标记数据提升驾驶场景识别模型性能，为自动驾驶中的模型压缩提供了可行方案。

search_text: Effectiveness of Function Matching in Driving Scene Recognition 功能匹配在驾驶场景识别中的有效性 Knowledge distillation is an effective approach for training compact recognizers required in autonomous driving. Recent studies on image classification have shown that matching student and teacher on a wide range of data points is critical for improving performance in distillation. This concept (called function matching) is suitable for driving scene recognition, where generally an almost infinite amount of unlabeled data are available. In this study, we experimentally investigate the impact of using such a large amount of unlabeled data for distillation on the performance of student models in structured prediction tasks for autonomous driving. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate that the performance of the compact student model can be improved dramatically and even match the performance of the large-scale teacher by knowledge distillation with massive unlabeled data. 知识蒸馏是训练自动驾驶所需的紧凑型识别器的有效方法。最近关于图像分类的研究表明，在广泛的数据点上匹配学生和教师对于提高蒸馏性能至关重要。这个概念（称为功能匹配）适用于驾驶场景识别，其中通常有几乎无限量的未标记数据可用。在本研究中，我们通过实验研究了使用如此大量的未标记数据进行蒸馏对自动驾驶结构化预测任务中的学生模型性能的影响。通过大量的实验，我们证明，通过大量未标记数据的知识蒸馏，紧凑学生模型的性能可以显着提高，甚至可以与大规模教师的性能相匹配。 本文研究了在自动驾驶场景识别中，通过知识蒸馏结合大量未标记数据进行函数匹配，以提升紧凑型学生模型性能的方法。 Shingo Yashima cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Wentao Chao,Xuechun Wang,Yingqian Wang,Guanghui Wang,Fuqing Duan
title: Learning Sub-Pixel Disparity Distribution for Light Field Depth Estimation
title_zh: 学习子像素视差分布以进行光场深度估计
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Light field (LF) depth estimation plays a crucial role in many LF-based applications. Existing LF depth estimation methods consider depth estimation as a regression problem, where a pixel-wise L1 loss is employed to supervise the training process. However, the disparity map is only a sub-space projection (i.e., an expectation) of the disparity distribution, which is essential for models to learn. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective method to learn the sub-pixel disparity distribution by fully utilizing the power of deep networks, especially for LF of narrow baselines. We construct the cost volume at the sub-pixel level to produce a finer disparity distribution and design an uncertainty-aware focal loss to supervise the predicted disparity distribution toward the ground truth. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.Our method significantly outperforms recent state-of-the-art LF depth algorithms on the HCI 4D LF Benchmark in terms of all four accuracy metrics (i.e., BadPix 0.01, BadPix 0.03, BadPix 0.07, and MSE $\times$100). The code and model of the proposed method are available at \url{https://github.com/chaowentao/SubFocal}.
summary: Light field (LF) depth estimation plays a crucial role in many LF-based applications. Existing LF depth estimation methods consider depth estimation as a regression problem, where a pixel-wise L1 loss is employed to supervise the training process. However, the disparity map is only a sub-space projection (i.e., an expectation) of the disparity distribution, which is essential for models to learn. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective method to learn the sub-pixel disparity distribution by fully utilizing the power of deep networks, especially for LF of narrow baselines. We construct the cost volume at the sub-pixel level to produce a finer disparity distribution and design an uncertainty-aware focal loss to supervise the predicted disparity distribution toward the ground truth. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.Our method significantly outperforms recent state-of-the-art LF depth algorithms on the HCI 4D LF Benchmark in terms of all four accuracy metrics (i.e., BadPix 0.01, BadPix 0.03, BadPix 0.07, and MSE $\times$100). The code and model of the proposed method are available at \url{https://github.com/chaowentao/SubFocal}.
abstract_zh: 光场 (LF) 深度估计在许多基于 LF 的应用中起着至关重要的作用。现有的 LF 深度估计方法将深度估计视为回归问题，其中采用逐像素 L1 损失来监督训练过程。然而，视差图只是视差分布的子空间投影（即期望），这对于模型的学习至关重要。在本文中，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法来充分利用深度网络的力量来学习子像素视差分布，特别是对于窄基线的LF。我们在子像素级别构建成本量，以产生更精细的视差分布，并设计一个不确定性感知的焦点损失，以监督预测的视差分布是否接近真实情况。大量的实验结果证明了我们方法的有效性。就所有四个精度指标（即 BadPix 0.01、BadPix 0.03、BadPix 0.07 和 MSE $\times$100）而言，我们的方法在 HCI 4D LF Benchmark 上显着优于最新的 LF 深度算法。该方法的代码和模型可在 \url{https://github.com/chaowentao/SubFocal} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种学习子像素视差分布的方法，用于改进光场深度估计，通过构建子像素级代价体和设计不确定性感知的焦点损失，提升深度估计精度。
AI.motivation: 现有光场深度估计方法将深度估计视为回归问题，仅利用像素级L1损失监督训练，无法充分学习视差分布信息，导致精度受限，尤其在窄基线光场场景下表现不足。
AI.method: 在子像素级别构建代价体以生成更精细的视差分布，并设计不确定性感知的焦点损失函数，引导模型学习真实视差分布，提升对窄基线光场的适应性。
AI.result: 在HCI 4D光场基准测试中，该方法在BadPix 0.01、BadPix 0.03、BadPix 0.07和MSE×100四项指标上均显著优于最新算法。
AI.conclusion: 通过建模子像素视差分布和优化损失函数，本文方法有效提升了光场深度估计的精度，尤其适用于窄基线场景，代码已开源。

Existing LF depth estimation methods consider depth estimation as a regression problem, where a pixel-wise L1 loss is employed to supervise the training process. However, the disparity map is only a sub-space projection (i.e., an expectation) of the disparity distribution, which is essential for models to learn. title: YOLOV: Making Still Image Object Detectors Great at Video Object Detection
title_zh: YOLO：使静态图像目标检测器在视频目标检测方面表现出色
abstract: Video object detection (VID) is challenging because of the high variation of object appearance as well as the diverse deterioration in some frames. On the positive side, the detection in a certain frame of a video, compared with that in a still image, can draw support from other frames. Hence, how to aggregate features across different frames is pivotal to VID problem. Most of existing aggregation algorithms are customized for two-stage detectors. However, these detectors are usually computationally expensive due to their two-stage nature. This work proposes a simple yet effective strategy to address the above concerns, which costs marginal overheads with significant gains in accuracy. Concretely, different from traditional two-stage pipeline, we select important regions after the one-stage detection to avoid processing massive low-quality candidates. Besides, we evaluate the relationship between a target frame and reference frames to guide the aggregation. We conduct extensive experiments and ablation studies to verify the efficacy of our design, and reveal its superiority over other state-of-the-art VID approaches in both effectiveness and efficiency. Our YOLOX-based model can achieve promising performance (\emph{e.g.}, 87.5\% AP50 at over 30 FPS on the ImageNet VID dataset on a single 2080Ti GPU), making it attractive for large-scale or real-time applications. The implementation is simple, we have made the demo codes and models available at \url{https://github.com/YuHengsss/YOLOV}.
summary: Video object detection (VID) is challenging because of the high variation of object appearance as well as the diverse deterioration in some frames. On the positive side, the detection in a certain frame of a video, compared with that in a still image, can draw support from other frames. Hence, how to aggregate features across different frames is pivotal to VID problem. Most of existing aggregation algorithms are customized for two-stage detectors. However, these detectors are usually computationally expensive due to their two-stage nature. This work proposes a simple yet effective strategy to address the above concerns, which costs marginal overheads with significant gains in accuracy. Concretely, different from traditional two-stage pipeline, we select important regions after the one-stage detection to avoid processing massive low-quality candidates. Besides, we evaluate the relationship between a target frame and reference frames to guide the aggregation. We conduct extensive experiments and ablation studies to verify the efficacy of our design, and reveal its superiority over other state-of-the-art VID approaches in both effectiveness and efficiency. Our YOLOX-based model can achieve promising performance (\emph{e.g.}, 87.5\% AP50 at over 30 FPS on the ImageNet VID dataset on a single 2080Ti GPU), making it attractive for large-scale or real-time applications. The implementation is simple, we have made the demo codes and models available at \url{https://github.com/YuHengsss/YOLOV}.
abstract_zh: 视频对象检测 (VID) 具有挑战性，因为对象外观变化很大，并且某些帧的恶化程度也多种多样。从积极的一面来看，视频某一帧中的检测与静止图像中的检测相比，可以得到其他帧的支持。因此，如何聚合不同帧的特征是 VID 问题的关键。大多数现有的聚合算法都是为两级检测器定制的。然而，由于这些检测器的两级性质，它们的计算成本通常很高。这项工作提出了一种简单而有效的策略来解决上述问题，该策略会减少边际开销，同时显着提高准确性。具体来说，与传统的两阶段管道不同，我们在一级检测后选择重要区域，以避免处理大量低质量候选区域。此外，我们评估目标帧和参考帧之间的关系以指导聚合。我们进行了广泛的实验和消融研究来验证我们设计的有效性，并揭示其在有效性和效率方面优于其他最先进的 VID 方法。我们基于 YOLOX 的模型可以实现令人鼓舞的性能（\emph{例如}，在单个 2080Ti GPU 上的 ImageNet VID 数据集上超过 30 FPS 时达到 87.5\% AP50），使其对大规模或实时应用程序具有吸引力。实现很简单，我们在 \url{https://github.com/YuHengsss/YOLOV} 提供了演示代码和模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出YOLOV方法，通过改进单阶段检测器使其在视频目标检测任务中表现优异。
AI.motivation: 视频目标检测面临帧间物体外观变化大、部分帧质量差的挑战，而现有特征聚合方法多针对计算成本高的两阶段检测器，缺乏高效解决方案。
AI.method: 在单阶段检测后筛选关键区域减少冗余计算，通过分析目标帧与参考帧的关系指导跨帧特征聚合，实现轻量级高效检测。
AI.result: 基于YOLOX的模型在ImageNet VID数据集上以30 FPS以上速度达到87.5% AP50，优于现有VID方法且代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: YOLOV在保持检测精度的同时显著提升效率，为实时视频检测提供了简单有效的解决方案。

search_text: YOLOV: Making Still Image Object Detectors Great at Video Object Detection YOLO：使静态图像目标检测器在视频目标检测方面表现出色 Video object detection (VID) is challenging because of the high variation of object appearance as well as the diverse deterioration in some frames. On the positive side, the detection in a certain frame of a video, compared with that in a still image, can draw support from other frames. Hence, how to aggregate features across different frames is pivotal to VID problem. Most of existing aggregation algorithms are customized for two-stage detectors. However, these detectors are usually computationally expensive due to their two-stage nature. This work proposes a simple yet effective strategy to address the above concerns, which costs marginal overheads with significant gains in accuracy. Concretely, different from traditional two-stage pipeline, we select important regions after the one-stage detection to avoid processing massive low-quality candidates. Besides, we evaluate the relationship between a target frame and reference frames to guide the aggregation. We conduct extensive experiments and ablation studies to verify the efficacy of our design, and reveal its superiority over other state-of-the-art VID approaches in both effectiveness and efficiency. Our YOLOX-based model can achieve promising performance (\emph{e.g.}, 87.5\% AP50 at over 30 FPS on the ImageNet VID dataset on a single 2080Ti GPU), making it attractive for large-scale or real-time applications. The implementation is simple, we have made the demo codes and models available at \url{https://github.com/YuHengsss/YOLOV}. 视频对象检测 (VID) 具有挑战性，因为对象外观变化很大，并且某些帧的恶化程度也多种多样。从积极的一面来看，视频某一帧中的检测与静止图像中的检测相比，可以得到其他帧的支持。因此，如何聚合不同帧的特征是 VID 问题的关键。大多数现有的聚合算法都是为两级检测器定制的。然而，由于这些检测器的两级性质，它们的计算成本通常很高。这项工作提出了一种简单而有效的策略来解决上述问题，该策略会减少边际开销，同时显着提高准确性。具体来说，与传统的两阶段管道不同，我们在一级检测后选择重要区域，以避免处理大量低质量候选区域。此外，我们评估目标帧和参考帧之间的关系以指导聚合。我们进行了广泛的实验和消融研究来验证我们设计的有效性，并揭示其在有效性和效率方面优于其他最先进的 VID 方法。我们基于 YOLOX 的模型可以实现令人鼓舞的性能（\emph{例如}，在单个 2080Ti GPU 上的 ImageNet VID 数据集上超过 30 FPS 时达到 87.5\% AP50），使其对大规模或实时应用程序具有吸引力。实现很简单，我们在 \url{https://github.com/YuHengsss/YOLOV} 提供了演示代码和模型。 本文提出YOLOV方法，通过改进单阶段检测器使其在视频目标检测任务中表现优异。 Yuheng Shi,Naiyan Wang,Xiaojie Guo cs.CV

title: Finding Emotions in Faces: A Meta-Classifier
title_zh: 寻找面部情绪：元分类器
abstract: Machine learning has been used to recognize emotions in faces, typically by looking for 8 different emotional states (neutral, happy, sad, surprise, fear, disgust, anger and contempt). We consider two approaches: feature recognition based on facial landmarks and deep learning on all pixels; each produced 58% overall accuracy. However, they produced different results on different images and thus we propose a new meta-classifier combining these approaches. It produces far better results with 77% accuracy
summary: Machine learning has been used to recognize emotions in faces, typically by looking for 8 different emotional states (neutral, happy, sad, surprise, fear, disgust, anger and contempt). We consider two approaches: feature recognition based on facial landmarks and deep learning on all pixels; each produced 58% overall accuracy. However, they produced different results on different images and thus we propose a new meta-classifier combining these approaches. It produces far better results with 77% accuracy
abstract_zh: 机器学习已被用来识别面部情绪，通常是通过寻找 8 种不同的情绪状态（中立、快乐、悲伤、惊讶、恐惧、厌恶、愤怒和蔑视）。我们考虑两种方法：基于面部标志的特征识别和对所有像素的深度学习；每个都产生 58% 的总体准确率。然而，它们在不同的图像上产生了不同的结果，因此我们提出了一种结合这些方法的新元分类器。它产生更好的结果，准确度为 77%

AI.tldr: 开发一种元分类器以提高面部情绪识别的准确率
AI.motivation: 现有方法在不同图像上表现不一致，需结合不同方法优势以提高准确率
AI.method: 结合面部关键点特征识别与深度学习方法，利用元分类器整合两种模型结果
AI.result: 元分类器在测试中达到77%的准确率，优于单一方法
AI.conclusion: 元分类器有效提升准确率，表明整合多方法可优化情绪识别性能

authors_text: Domenic Bersch,Kshitij Dwivedi,Martina Vilas,Radoslaw M. Cichy,Gemma Roig
title: Net2Brain: A Toolbox to compare artificial vision models with human brain responses
title_zh: Net2Brain：比较人工视觉模型与人脑反应的工具箱
comment: Published in Frontiers in Neuroinformatics (2025), Article 1515873. Version of record: https://doi.org/10.3389/fninf.2025.1515873 4 Pages, 3 figures, submitted and accepted to CCNeuro 2022. For associated repository, see https://github.com/ToastyDom/Net2Brain Update 1: Changed Citation
comments: Published in Frontiers in Neuroinformatics (2025), Article 1515873. Version of record: https://doi.org/10.3389/fninf.2025.1515873 4 Pages, 3 figures, submitted and accepted to CCNeuro 2022. For associated repository, see https://github.com/ToastyDom/Net2Brain Update 1: Changed Citation
abstract: We introduce Net2Brain, a graphical and command-line user interface toolbox for comparing the representational spaces of artificial deep neural networks (DNNs) and human brain recordings. While different toolboxes facilitate only single functionalities or only focus on a small subset of supervised image classification models, Net2Brain allows the extraction of activations of more than 600 DNNs trained to perform a diverse range of vision-related tasks (e.g semantic segmentation, depth estimation, action recognition, etc.), over both image and video datasets. The toolbox computes the representational dissimilarity matrices (RDMs) over those activations and compares them to brain recordings using representational similarity analysis (RSA), weighted RSA, both in specific ROIs and with searchlight search. In addition, it is possible to add a new data set of stimuli and brain recordings to the toolbox for evaluation. We demonstrate the functionality and advantages of Net2Brain with an example showcasing how it can be used to test hypotheses of cognitive computational neuroscience.
summary: We introduce Net2Brain, a graphical and command-line user interface toolbox for comparing the representational spaces of artificial deep neural networks (DNNs) and human brain recordings. While different toolboxes facilitate only single functionalities or only focus on a small subset of supervised image classification models, Net2Brain allows the extraction of activations of more than 600 DNNs trained to perform a diverse range of vision-related tasks (e.g semantic segmentation, depth estimation, action recognition, etc.), over both image and video datasets. The toolbox computes the representational dissimilarity matrices (RDMs) over those activations and compares them to brain recordings using representational similarity analysis (RSA), weighted RSA, both in specific ROIs and with searchlight search. In addition, it is possible to add a new data set of stimuli and brain recordings to the toolbox for evaluation. We demonstrate the functionality and advantages of Net2Brain with an example showcasing how it can be used to test hypotheses of cognitive computational neuroscience.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍 Net2Brain，这是一个图形和命令行用户界面工具箱，用于比较人工深度神经网络 (DNN) 和人脑记录的表示空间。虽然不同的工具箱仅支持单一功能或仅关注监督图像分类模型的一小部分，但 Net2Brain 允许提取 600 多个经过训练的 DNN 的激活，以在图像和视频数据集上执行各种视觉相关任务（例如语义分割、深度估计、动作识别等）。该工具箱计算这些激活的表征相异矩阵 (RDM)，并使用表征相似性分析 (RSA)、加权 RSA（在特定 ROI 中和探照灯搜索）将它们与大脑记录进行比较。此外，还可以将新的刺激和大脑记录数据集添加到工具箱中进行评估。我们通过一个示例展示了 Net2Brain 的功能和优势，展示了如何使用它来测试认知计算神经科学的假设。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为Net2Brain的工具箱，用于比较人工深度神经网络（DNNs）与人类大脑反应的表示空间。
AI.motivation: 现有工具功能单一，仅支持少数监督图像分类模型，缺乏对多样化视觉任务和大规模DNN模型的比较能力。
AI.method: 开发图形界面和命令行工具，提取600+视觉任务DNN的激活数据，计算代表差异矩阵（RDMs），并通过RSA等方法与大脑记录进行比较，支持自定义数据集添加。
AI.result: 通过实例验证了Net2Brain在比较人工模型与大脑反应中的有效性，展示了其在认知计算神经科学假设检验中的应用潜力。
AI.conclusion: Net2Brain为跨模态比较人工视觉模型与人类大脑反应提供了高效工具，推动了对视觉处理机制的理解。

While different toolboxes facilitate only single functionalities or only focus on a small subset of supervised image classification models, Net2Brain allows the extraction of activations of more than 600 DNNs trained to perform a diverse range of vision-related tasks (e.g semantic segmentation, depth estimation, action recognition, etc.), over both image and video datasets. In addition, it is possible to add a new data set of stimuli and brain recordings to the toolbox for evaluation.
